rationale: This structure acknowledges the current dominance of AI/ML by splitting it into four distinct, high-volume specialties (Machine Learning, Computer Vision, NLP, Robotics) plus a category for classic AI, preventing any single one from becoming too coarse. It balances these with foundational and systems-oriented areas of computer science, treating fields like Systems, Theory, Security, and Programming Languages as first-class peers, which reflects the structure of the field in both academia and industry. We deliberately kept Machine Learning as a broad category, rather than splitting out sub-fields like Reinforcement Learning, to avoid over-fragmentation. The total count of 14 provides comprehensive coverage without overwhelming the user.

subspecialties.name: Machine Learning
subspecialties.tagline: Core algorithms, deep learning, reinforcement learning, and ML theory.
covers: Developing and analyzing algorithms that learn from data.
Neural network architectures, optimization methods, statistical learning theory, reinforcement learning, representation learning.
explicitly_excludes: Applications focused on images/video (→ Computer Vision) or text/speech (→ NLP).

subspecialties.name: Computer Vision
subspecialties.tagline: Enabling computers to see and interpret the visual world.
covers: Processing, analyzing, and understanding images and videos.
Object detection, image recognition, 3D reconstruction, generative models for images, semantic segmentation.
explicitly_excludes: Core rendering techniques (→ Computer Graphics & Visualization), vision for robotics (→ Robotics).

subspecialties.name: Natural Language Processing
subspecialties.tagline: Teaching computers to understand, process, and generate human language.
covers: Algorithms for text and speech data.
Large language models (LLMs), machine translation, sentiment analysis, speech recognition, information extraction.
explicitly_excludes: Information retrieval and search engine architecture (→ Databases & Information Retrieval).

subspecialties.name: Systems & Networking
subspecialties.tagline: Operating systems, distributed systems, cloud computing, and network protocols.
covers: Design, implementation, and analysis of complex computing systems.
Operating systems, distributed consensus, cloud infrastructure, network architecture, mobile systems.
explicitly_excludes: Database-specific systems (→ Databases & Information Retrieval), security of systems (→ Security & Privacy).

subspecialties.name: Security & Privacy
subspecialties.tagline: Cryptography, system security, and the protection of digital information.
covers: Protecting systems, networks, and data from attack, damage, or unauthorized access.
Cryptography, network security, software security, privacy-enhancing technologies, formal verification.
explicitly_excludes: Core networking protocols (→ Systems & Networking), ethical implications of AI (often falls under application area).

subspecialties.name: Theory & Algorithms
subspecialties.tagline: The fundamental mathematical foundations of computation and algorithm design.
covers: Formal models of computation and the design and analysis of efficient algorithms.
Computational complexity, algorithm design, data structures, cryptography theory, quantum computing theory.
explicitly_excludes: Analysis of machine learning algorithms (→ Machine Learning), programming language theory (→ Programming Languages).

subspecialties.name: Robotics
subspecialties.tagline: The science and engineering of autonomous physical agents.
covers: Design, control, perception, and planning for robots.
Motion planning, kinematics, control systems, simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM), human-robot interaction.
explicitly_excludes: Purely vision-based perception (→ Computer Vision), core reinforcement learning algorithms (→ Machine Learning).

subspecialties.name: Human-Computer Interaction (HCI)
subspecialties.tagline: Designing and studying the interface between people and computers.
covers: The design, evaluation, and implementation of interactive computing systems for human use.
User interface design, user experience (UX) research, usability studies, accessibility, social computing.
explicitly_excludes: Rendering and modeling techniques (→ Computer Graphics & Visualization), human-robot interaction (→ Robotics).

subspecialties.name: Databases & Information Retrieval
subspecialties.tagline: Managing, querying, and finding information in large-scale data systems.
covers: Principles of data management systems and information access.
Database systems (SQL/NoSQL), query optimization, data warehousing, search engine technology, data mining.
explicitly_excludes: Distributed systems infrastructure (→ Systems & Networking), NLP for text understanding (→ Natural Language Processing).

subspecialties.name: Computer Graphics & Visualization
subspecialties.tagline: Generating images, animations, and interactive visual representations of data.
covers: Synthesis of visual content and methods for representing data visually.
Real-time rendering, physical simulation, computational geometry, scientific visualization, virtual reality.
explicitly_excludes: Analysis of existing images (→ Computer Vision), user interface design (→ Human-Computer Interaction).

subspecialties.name: Artificial Intelligence & Reasoning
subspecialties.tagline: Symbolic reasoning, knowledge representation, search, and planning.
covers: The study of intelligent agents and environments, often with a focus on symbolic methods.
Knowledge representation, automated planning, search algorithms, multi-agent systems, logic-based AI.
explicitly_excludes: Data-driven and statistical learning approaches (→ Machine Learning).

subspecialties.name: Software Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: The systematic design, development, testing, and maintenance of software.
covers: Principles and practices for building and evolving large-scale software systems.
Software development methodologies, automated testing, program analysis, software architecture, requirements engineering.
explicitly_excludes: Compiler design and language theory (→ Programming Languages), formal verification for security (→ Security & Privacy).

subspecialties.name: Programming Languages
subspecialties.tagline: The design, theory, and implementation of programming languages.
covers: Formal study of programming languages and their execution.
Language design, compiler construction, type theory, formal methods, program verification.
explicitly_excludes: Empirical studies of programmers (→ Software Engineering), low-level system implementation (→ Systems & Networking).

subspecialties.name: Computational Science & Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Using high-performance computing to simulate and solve scientific problems.
covers: Application of computer science to problems in science and engineering.
High-performance computing (HPC), numerical methods, scientific simulation, computational physics/chemistry.
explicitly_excludes: Core computer systems research (→ Systems & Networking), computational biology (→ Biology level-1).

uncovered_concerns: AI Ethics & Safety was considered but not included as a separate category because it is highly cross-cutting; papers are better classified by their primary application domain (e.g., fairness in NLP).
Quantum Computing theory is covered under 'Theory & Algorithms', while hardware aspects are out of scope for this level-1. It is not yet a large enough paper sub-field to warrant its own category.
Data Mining is largely subsumed by modern 'Machine Learning' and 'Databases & Information Retrieval', so it was not given a standalone category.
